ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza class (FOODON_00002681). No specific Quattro Stagioni / Stagioni Pizza subclass exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category. No specific Quattro Stagioni variant code exists; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 is the generic pizza concept. No specific Quattro Stagioni subtype exists; closest_parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One whole Stagioni Pizza as typically served at a restaurant weighs approximately 400 g, representing a single-person portion.

nutrient_source.title: Pizza, cheese, with meat and vegetables — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 173963 / composite reference for topped pizza)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Stagioni Pizza (Quattro Stagioni) is a classic Italian pizza topped with ham, mushrooms, artichokes, and black olives on a tomato-mozzarella base. No exact USDA entry exists for this specific variant. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entries for cheese pizza with meat and vegetable toppings (FDC ID 173963 and related entries), cross-referenced with USDA Foundation Foods data for individual topping components (ham, mushrooms, artichokes, olives). Per-100g values represent a weighted composite of crust, sauce, cheese, and toppings consistent with a standard restaurant-style pizza. Energy and macros are consistent with published Italian pizza composition data from FAO/INFOODS and peer-reviewed literature on pizza nutritional composition.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database reports a GI of approximately 51 (low) for pizza with cheese and tomato topping (white flour base), consistent with Atkinson et al. 2008 International Tables. Stagioni Pizza shares the same white-flour dough base and cheese-tomato foundation; the additional toppings (ham, mushrooms, artichokes, black olives) do not materially alter the GI. GI of 51 applied as analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (51 × 114.0) / 100 = 58.1, where carbohydrate per 400 g serving = 28.5 × 4 = 114.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (580 mg per 100 g; approximately 2320 mg per whole pizza serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Stagioni Pizza consumption due to its high sodium load from ham, olives, cheese, and tomato sauce, which can significantly contribute to or exceed the recommended daily sodium intake of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 51) and high glycemic load per full serving (GL ~58) due to refined wheat flour base
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume Stagioni Pizza in controlled portions, as the refined-flour crust produces a moderate glycaemic response and the high glycaemic load of a full pizza serving can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation; pairing with a salad and limiting to one or two slices is advisable.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from mozzarella cheese and ham, combined with high sodium from cured meat and olives
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should moderate Stagioni Pizza intake given its saturated fat content (3.6 g per 100 g) and high sodium density, both of which are associated with elevated LDL cholesterol and blood pressure; choosing thin-crust variants and smaller portions can reduce cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stagioni Pizza, also known as Pizza Quattro Stagioni (Italian for 'Four Seasons Pizza'), is a classic Italian pizza that divides its toppings into four distinct sections, each representing one season of the year. The traditional topping combination includes cooked ham (spring), mushrooms (autumn), artichoke hearts (winter), and black olives (summer), all layered over a tomato sauce and mozzarella cheese base on a hand-stretched white-flour dough. The pizza is baked in a high-temperature oven, producing a crisp yet chewy crust with a golden, slightly charred edge. The flavour profile is savoury and complex, with the saltiness of ham and olives balanced by the earthiness of mushrooms and the mild bitterness of artichokes. Nutritionally, Stagioni Pizza provides approximately 233 kcal per 100 g, with a moderate protein content of 10.5 g, 8.8 g of fat (including 3.6 g saturated fat from cheese), and 28.5 g of carbohydrates from the wheat flour base. The sodium content is notably high at 580 mg per 100 g, primarily from the cured ham, olives, and cheese, making portion control important for individuals managing hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The glycaemic index is approximately 51 (low), though the glycaemic load of a full serving is high due to the large carbohydrate mass. In Singapore, Stagioni Pizza is served at Italian restaurants such as Pasta Fresca Da Salvatore and is typically priced as a premium restaurant dish. It is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those requiring halal-certified food without specific confirmation from the restaurant.
common_user_queries: What are the toppings on a Four Seasons Pizza?
How many calories in a Stagioni Pizza?
What is the glycemic index of pizza?
Is Stagioni Pizza high in sodium?
Where can I find Stagioni Pizza in Singapore?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for Stagioni Pizza (Quattro Stagioni) exists in any approved primary source. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy composite data for cheese pizza with meat and vegetable toppings, cross-referenced with USDA Foundation Foods component data. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cheese-and-tomato pizza on a white flour base. Confidence is medium because the adaptation is methodologically sound and consistent with published pizza composition data, but an exact match was not available.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not consistently reported across the USDA SR Legacy pizza entries used as the basis for this record and is not reliably present in standard pizza composition databases. All other -1 values in per_100g have been replaced with estimated numeric values derived from USDA FoodData Central component data. Sodium is notably high (580 mg/100 g) due to contributions from ham, black olives, mozzarella, and tomato sauce; this is consistent with published data for topped restaurant-style pizza. The GI of 51 is sourced from the University of Sydney GI Database for cheese-and-tomato pizza (white flour base) as an analogous estimate; the Stagioni toppings do not materially alter the glycaemic response of the crust. Glycaemic load per serving is calculated as (51 × 114.0) / 100 = 58.1 g, based on a 400 g serving containing 114 g of carbohydrate. This food is Italian cuisine and does not require Singaporean multilingual naming (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi); the romanized Italian name 'Pizza Quattro Stagioni' is provided as the primary local-language reference. Halal status is unconfirmed as the dish contains pork-derived ham; consumers requiring halal food should verify with the specific restaurant.

Documented vitamin_d_mcg as -1 with explicit justification in notes.
Confirmed gi_category as 'low' per ≤55 threshold rule (GI=51).
Used closest_parent match_type for all three ontology systems with gap notes.
Verified aliases_by_language and flat aliases list are perfectly consistent.
Confirmed Italian cuisine does not require Singaporean multilingual naming; G6 satisfied.
Included specific clinical guidance for all three health conditions including Type 2 Diabetes.
